# Customer Churn Predictor — AI Context

## Overview
Flask web app that predicts telecom customer churn using a Random Forest model with SHAP-based per-customer explanations.

## Key Files
- `app.py` — Flask routes (GET/POST /, /api/predict, /health) + encoding logic + SHAP explainer
- `templates/home.html` — Jinja2 form with 19 fields, colored result banner, SHAP factors
- `model.sav` — Pickled RandomForestClassifier (trained on 7,047 tel_churn.csv records)
- `tel_churn.csv` — One-hot encoded training data (50 feature columns)

## Architecture
- Model loaded once at module level (`pickle.load('model.sav')`)
- Custom `encode_input()` maps raw form values to one-hot columns via dict
- SHAP TreeExplainer generates per-customer top-3 factors
- Dual interface: form POST for humans, JSON POST for /api/predict

## Key Decisions
- Dict-based one-hot encoding (not pd.get_dummies) to ensure exact column match
- THRESHOLD=0.35 (not default 0.5) for business sensitivity
- Confidence = prob_churn when predicting churn, 1-prob_churn when predicting stay
- Human-readable FEATURE_DISPLAY map for all 50 ML columns

## Features to Maintain
- Input validation: float/int coercion with try/except
- Loading state on submit button (disabled + "Predicting...")
- fmt_num() strips .0 from whole numbers; fmt_pct() strips .00 from confidence
- .form-label class on all labels (no cascade inheritance)

## Deployment
- Render: gunicorn app:app
- Health check: /health
- No env vars or database required
